decisions.name: Microservices Architecture
decisions.rationale: To achieve scalability, real-time collaboration, and low-latency, we will adopt a microservices architecture. Each service will be responsible for a specific feature or functionality, allowing for independent deployment, scaling, and maintenance.

decisions.name: Service-Oriented Design
decisions.rationale: We will apply service-oriented design principles to ensure loose coupling between services, enabling flexibility and scalability. Each service will have a clear interface and will be designed to be stateless, making it easier to scale and manage.

decisions.name: Containerization
decisions.rationale: To ensure consistency and portability across environments, we will use containerization (e.g., Docker) to package and deploy our services. This will also enable efficient resource utilization and simplify service deployment and management.

decisions.name: API Gateway
decisions.rationale: To manage incoming requests and provide a single entry point for our services, we will implement an API Gateway. This will enable us to handle authentication, rate limiting, and request routing, while also providing a unified interface for our services.

decisions.name: Service Discovery
decisions.rationale: To enable dynamic service discovery and registration, we will implement a service discovery mechanism (e.g., etcd or Consul). This will allow our services to register themselves and be discovered by other services, making it easier to manage and scale our system.

decisions.name: Monitoring and Logging
decisions.rationale: To ensure the system is operating correctly and to identify issues, we will implement monitoring and logging mechanisms (e.g., Prometheus, Grafana, and ELK Stack). This will provide us with real-time insights into our system's performance and help us troubleshoot issues.

decisions.name: Security
decisions.rationale: To ensure the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of user data, we will implement robust security measures, including authentication, authorization, and encryption. We will also follow secure coding practices and conduct regular security audits to identify and address potential vulnerabilities.

decisions.name: Threading and Multiprocessing
decisions.rationale: To improve system performance and responsiveness, we will use threading and multiprocessing to execute tasks concurrently. This will enable us to take advantage of multi-core processors and improve the overall system responsiveness.

decisions.name: YOLOv5 Model for Face Detection
decisions.rationale: To detect the user's face, we will use the YOLOv5 model, which is a state-of-the-art object detection algorithm. This will provide us with accurate and efficient face detection capabilities.

decisions.name: Background Subtraction for Hand Tracking
decisions.rationale: To track the user's hand movements, we will use background subtraction, which is a technique that separates the moving parts from the background. This will enable us to accurately track the user's hand movements and provide a seamless user experience.

decisions.name: Histogram Equalization for Image Contrast Optimization
decisions.rationale: To optimize the image contrast, we will use histogram equalization, which is a technique that adjusts the image contrast to make it more visually appealing. This will improve the overall user experience and provide a more engaging interface.

components.name: Camera
components.responsibility: Captures hand gesture commands and the user's facial features as input to the system.

components.name: YOLOv5 Model
components.responsibility: Detects the user's face using the YOLOv5 model.

components.name: Background Subtraction (BS)
components.responsibility: Separates the moving parts (e.g., user's hand movement) from the background.

components.name: Threading
components.responsibility: Enables concurrent execution of tasks within a single process.

components.name: Multiprocessing
components.responsibility: Enables parallel execution of tasks across multiple processes and is beneficial for data-intensive operations.

components.name: Face Recognition
components.responsibility: Recognizes and identifies the user's face.

components.name: Single-Hand Interaction
components.responsibility: Allows the user to interact with the system using a single hand.

components.name: Custom Gesture Actions
components.responsibility: Customizes the actions for specific gestures.

critique.description: Data Flow from YOLOv5 Model to Background Subtraction (BS) is unclear.
Real-time collaboration and scalability are not explicitly handled by the system.
Low-latency and data flow from Gesture Recognition to Custom Gesture Actions are unclear.
Usability and Single-Hand Interaction are not explicitly handled by the system.
Security and data flow are not explicitly handled by the system.
Competitive price and hardware limitations are not explicitly handled by the system.
